input_arguments.name: overwrite

input_arguments.name: zero-for-flat

input_arguments.name: no-edges
input_arguments.description: Do not try to interpolate values at dataset edges or close to nodata values

input_arguments.name: overwrite
input_arguments.description: Whether overwriting existing output is allowed

input_arguments.name: no-check-srs
input_arguments.description: Do not check consistency of input spatial reference systems

input_arguments.name: no-check-extent
input_arguments.description: Do not check consistency of input extents

sub_algorithms.name: clean-collar
sub_algorithms.description: Clean the collar of a raster dataset, removing noise.

input_arguments.name: overwrite
input_arguments.description: Whether overwriting existing output is allowed

input_arguments.name: update
input_arguments.description: Whether to open existing dataset in update mode

input_arguments.name: color-threshold
input_arguments.description: Select how far from specified transparent colors the pixel values are considered transparent.

input_arguments.name: pixel-distance
input_arguments.description: Number of consecutive transparent pixels that can be encountered before the giving up search inwards.

input_arguments.name: add-alpha
input_arguments.description: Adds an alpha band to the output dataset.

input_arguments.name: add-mask
input_arguments.description: Adds a mask band to the output dataset.

input_arguments.name: like-layer
input_arguments.description: Name of the layer of the 'like' dataset

input_arguments.name: like-where

input_arguments.name: only-bbox
input_arguments.description: For 'geometry' and 'like', only consider their bounding box

input_arguments.name: allow-bbox-outside-source
input_arguments.description: Allow clipping box to include pixels outside input dataset

input_arguments.name: add-alpha
input_arguments.description: Adds an alpha mask band to the destination when the source raster have none.

input_arguments.description: Name of the maximum elevation field

input_arguments.name: 3d
input_arguments.description: Force production of 3D vectors instead of 2D

input_arguments.name: src-nodata
input_arguments.description: Input pixel value to treat as 'nodata'

input_arguments.name: exp-base
input_arguments.description: Base for exponential contour level generation

input_arguments.name: offset
input_arguments.description: Offset to apply to contour levels

input_arguments.description: Whether overwriting existing output is allowed

sub_algorithms.description: Create a new raster dataset.

input_arguments.name: overwrite
input_arguments.description: Whether overwriting existing output is allowed

input_arguments.name: append
input_arguments.description: Append as a subdataset to existing output

input_arguments.name: nodata
input_arguments.description: Assign a specified nodata value to output bands ('none', numeric value, 'nan', 'inf', '-inf')

input_arguments.name: copy-overviews
input_arguments.description: Create same overview levels as input dataset

sub_algorithms.name: edit
sub_algorithms.description: Edit a raster dataset.

input_arguments.name: auxiliary
input_arguments.description: Ask for an auxiliary .aux.xml file to be edited

input_arguments.name: nodata
input_arguments.description: Assign a specified nodata value to output bands ('none', numeric value, 'nan', 'inf', '-inf')

input_arguments.name: approx-stats
input_arguments.description: Compute statistics, using a subset of pixels

sub_algorithms.name: fill-nodata
sub_algorithms.description: Fill nodata raster regions by interpolation from edges.

input_arguments.name: overwrite
input_arguments.description: Whether overwriting existing output is allowed

input_arguments.name: max-distance
input_arguments.description: The maximum distance (in pixels) that the algorithm will search out for values to interpolate.

input_arguments.name: smoothing-iterations
input_arguments.description: The number of 3x3 average filter smoothing iterations to run after the interpolation to dampen artifacts. The default is zero smoothing iterations.

input_arguments.name: mask
input_arguments.description: Use the first band of the specified file as a validity mask (zero is invalid, non-zero is valid).

input_arguments.name: strategy
input_arguments.description: By default, pixels are interpolated using an inverse distance weighting (invdist). It is also possible to choose a nearest neighbour (nearest) strategy.

sub_algorithms.name: footprint
sub_algorithms.description: Compute the footprint of a raster dataset.

input_arguments.name: append
input_arguments.description: Whether to append to an existing dataset

input_arguments.name: overwrite
input_arguments.description: Whether overwriting existing output is allowed

input_arguments.name: combine-bands
input_arguments.description: Defines how the mask bands of the selected bands are combined to generate a single mask band, before being vectorized.

input_arguments.name: overview
input_arguments.description: Which overview level of source file must be used

input_arguments.name: src-nodata
input_arguments.description: Set nodata values for input bands.

input_arguments.name: split-multipolygons
input_arguments.description: Whether to split multipolygons as several features each with one single polygon

input_arguments.name: convex-hull
input_arguments.description: Whether to compute the convex hull of the footprint

input_arguments.name: densify-distance
input_arguments.description: Maximum distance between 2 consecutive points of the output geometry.

input_arguments.name: simplify-tolerance
input_arguments.description: Tolerance used to merge consecutive points of the output geometry.

input_arguments.name: min-ring-area
input_arguments.description: Minimum value for the area of a ring

input_arguments.name: max-points
input_arguments.description: Maximum number of points of each output geometry

input_arguments.name: location-field
input_arguments.description: Name of the field where the path of the input dataset will be stored.

input_arguments.name: no-location-field
input_arguments.description: Disable creating a field with the path of the input dataset

input_arguments.name: absolute-path
input_arguments.description: Whether the path to the input dataset should be stored as an absolute path

sub_algorithms.name: hillshade
sub_algorithms.description: Generate a shaded relief map

input_arguments.name: overwrite
input_arguments.description: Whether overwriting existing output is allowed

input_arguments.name: zfactor
input_arguments.description: Vertical exaggeration used to pre-multiply the elevations

input_arguments.name: xscale
input_arguments.description: Ratio of vertical units to horizontal X axis units

input_arguments.name: yscale
input_arguments.description: Ratio of vertical units to horizontal Y axis units

input_arguments.name: azimuth
input_arguments.description: Azimuth of the light, in degrees

input_arguments.name: altitude
input_arguments.description: Altitude of the light, in degrees

input_arguments.name: gradient-alg
input_arguments.description: Algorithm used to compute terrain gradient

input_arguments.name: no-edges
input_arguments.description: Do not try to interpolate values at dataset edges or close to nodata values

sub_algorithms.name: index
sub_algorithms.description: Create a vector index of raster datasets.

input_arguments.name: overwrite
input_arguments.description: Whether overwriting existing output is allowed

input_arguments.name: update
input_arguments.description: Whether to open existing dataset in update mode

input_arguments.name: overwrite-layer
input_arguments.description: Whether overwriting existing layer is allowed

input_arguments.name: append
input_arguments.description: Whether appending to existing layer is allowed

input_arguments.name: recursive
input_arguments.description: Whether input directories should be explored recursively.

input_arguments.name: filename-filter
input_arguments.description: Pattern that the filenames in input directories should follow ('*' and '?' wildcard)

input_arguments.name: min-pixel-size
input_arguments.description: Minimum pixel size in term of geospatial extent per pixel (resolution) that a raster should have to be selected.

input_arguments.name: max-pixel-size
input_arguments.description: Maximum pixel size in term of geospatial extent per pixel (resolution) that a raster should have to be selected.

input_arguments.name: location-name
input_arguments.description: Name of the field with the raster path

input_arguments.name: absolute-path
input_arguments.description: Whether the path to the input datasets should be stored as an absolute path

input_arguments.name: source-crs-field-name
input_arguments.description: Name of the field to store the CRS of each dataset

input_arguments.name: source-crs-format
input_arguments.description: Format in which the CRS of each dataset must be written

sub_algorithms.name: info
sub_algorithms.description: Return information on a raster dataset.

input_arguments.name: stats
input_arguments.description: Retrieve or compute statistics, using all pixels

input_arguments.name: approx-stats
input_arguments.description: Retrieve or compute statistics, using a subset of pixels

input_arguments.name: no-gcp
input_arguments.description: Suppress ground control points list printing

input_arguments.name: list-mdd
input_arguments.description: List all metadata domains available for the dataset

input_arguments.name: metadata-domain
input_arguments.description: Report metadata for the specified domain. 'all' can be used to report metadata in all domains

input_arguments.name: subdataset
input_arguments.description: Use subdataset of specified index (starting at 1), instead of the source dataset itself

input_arguments.name: stdout
input_arguments.description: Directly output on stdout (format=text mode only). If enabled, output-string will be empty

output_arguments.name: output-string
output_arguments.description: Output string, in which the result is placed

input_arguments.description: Tile numbering convention: xyz (from top) or tms (from bottom)

input_arguments.name: add-alpha
input_arguments.description: Whether to force adding an alpha channel

input_arguments.name: no-alpha
input_arguments.description: Whether to disable adding an alpha channel

input_arguments.name: copy-src-metadata
input_arguments.description: Whether to copy metadata from source dataset

input_arguments.description: Tuples of values (e.g. <R>,<G>,<B> or (<R1>,<G1>,<B1>),(<R2>,<G2>,<B2>)) that must beignored as contributing source pixels during (average) resampling

input_arguments.name: excluded-values-pct-threshold
input_arguments.description: Minimum percentage of source pixels that must be set at one of the --excluded-values to cause the excluded value to be used as the target pixel value

input_arguments.name: nodata-values-pct-threshold
input_arguments.description: Minimum percentage of source pixels that must be set at one of nodata (or alpha=0 or any other way to express transparent pixelto cause the target pixel value to be transparent

input_arguments.description: URL address where the generated tiles are going to be published

input_arguments.description: Filename of a template mapml file where variables will be substituted

sub_algorithms.name: viewshed
sub_algorithms.description: Compute the viewshed of a raster dataset.

input_arguments.name: overwrite
input_arguments.description: Whether overwriting existing output is allowed

input_arguments.name: target-height
input_arguments.description: Height of the target above the DEM surface in the height unit of the DEM.

input_arguments.name: mode
input_arguments.description: Sets what information the output contains.

input_arguments.name: max-distance
input_arguments.description: Maximum distance from observer to compute visibility. It is also used to clamp the extent of the output raster.

input_arguments.name: curvature-coefficient
input_arguments.description: Coefficient to consider the effect of the curvature and refraction.

input_arguments.name: visible-value
input_arguments.description: Pixel value to set for visible areas

input_arguments.name: invisible-value
input_arguments.description: Pixel value to set for invisible areas

input_arguments.name: out-of-range-value
input_arguments.description: Pixel value to set for the cells that fall outside of the range specified by the observer location and the maximum distance

input_arguments.name: dst-nodata
input_arguments.description: The value to be set for the cells in the output raster that have no data.

input_arguments.name: drivers
input_arguments.description: Display raster driver list as JSON document

output_arguments.name: output-string
output_arguments.description: Output string, in which the result is placed
